Ptilotus is a genus of flowering plants in the family Amaranthaceae, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ptilotus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ptilotus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
